The Commissioner of Planning and the members of the Planning Board, Soil and Water Conservation Board, Housing Opportunity Commission
and Historic Preservation Advisory Committee are appointed by the County Executive, subject to confirmation by the Board of Legislators.
Members of the Urban County Council and the Community Development Advisory Group are appointed by the local municipalities. Members of
the Agriculture and Farmland Board are appointed by the Board of Legislators.
C-173

Department Of Planning (19)
Mission Statement
The mission of the Department of Planning is to shape and influence growth and development in Westchester County in order to improve
quality of life and protect the environment, resulting in more livable and sustainable communities.

Westchester Urban County Community $9,179,126 $4,330,334 $4,330,334
Development Program
Acct T-067 19-067W. Granting Agency: US Dept. of Housing & Urban Development. This is a grant-in-aid program whereby the County and 25 participating
communities can undertake essential community development activities such as public works, neighborhood revitalization, housing rehabilitation, flood control
improvements and administrative support to public service organizations. Project funding will be provided to municipal or non-profit sponsors. Housing units will be
rehabilitated and physical improvement projects will receive technical design assistance. Term: 05/01/21 - 04/30/22. Positions: 8.
Prior Year Current Year Federal State Adv. to Grants Other
HOME Program $957,156 $5,110,114 $5,110,114
Acct T-173 19-173W. Granting Agency: US Dept. of Housing & Urban Development. A Grant to provide funds for new construction and rehabilitation to developers and
not-for-profit organizations to develop housing for low income families as authorized by the National Affordable Housing Act, 1990. Various additional units of affordable
housing will be constructed/rehabilitated. Term: 05/01/21 - 04/30/22.
Prior Year Current Year Federal State Adv. to Grants Other
Emergency Solutions Grant $5,554,016 $316,080 $316,080
Acct T-215 19-215W. Granting Agency: US Dept. of Housing & Urban Development. A grant providing funds for projects which assist communities and not-for-profit
organizations in providing housing and programs for the homeless. Funds will be used for eviction prevention to assist families, rehabilitation of facilities containing
homeless shelter beds and counseling for homeless individuals, including children. Term: 05/01/21 - 04/30/22.

AHC Program $780,000 $780,000
Acct T-085 19-085V. Funding Source: NYSAHC. The New York State Affordable Housing Corporation (?AHC?) awarded funding to be used for home improvements. The
County will leverage this with CDBG and/or LSW funds to provide a total of $975,000 for a period of four years to assist 26 homeowners with home renovations. Focus
of the funds will be certain neighborhoods in Mt. Vernon, Peekskill, New Rochelle, White Plains, Yonkers, Mamaroneck, Port Chester and Ossining. Term: 04/01/20 - 12/
01/23.

Lead Based Paint Hazard Reduction Grant $4,100,000 $4,100,000 $4,100,000
Acct T-471 19-471W. Granting Agency: US Dept. of Housing & Urban Development. This award will be used to provide grants to remediate lead-based paint hazards in
approximately 240 privately-owned housing units, including units where tenants receive Housing Choice Voucher assistance. A portion of this grant will be used to
provide education and outreach for the Lead Safe Westchester (LSW) program as well as lead hazard remediation training in safe work practices for low and moderate
income persons from neighborhoods to work under expert supervision where multiple cases of lead poisoning have been reported. Term: 09/01/21 - 04/01/25. Positions:
included in the Operating Budget.

Soil and Water Conservation District $160,000 $175,000 $175,000
Acct T-801 19-801G. Granting Agency: Various Agencies including NYSDEC. The WCSWCD facilitates programs that promote soil and water conservation including
Phase II stormwater implementation, agricultural environmental management and watershed protection and management. The District has been receiving small grants
through various agencies to implement Soil and Water conservation, stormwater and management practices including MS4 permit requirements. Term: 01/1/06 - On-
going. Positions: included in the Operating Budget.

Department Of Planning (19)
Account Title Program Amount Program Amount Current Year Program Amount: Funding Breakdown
Prior Year Current Year Federal State Adv. to Grants Other
Climate Resilient Farming Program $89,851 $89,851
Acct G-019 19-V019. Granting Agency: NYSDAM. The Westchester County SWCD is partnering with the Watershed Agricultural Council (WAC) on a NYS Climate
Resilient Farming Program $89,851 grant from the NYS Department of Agriculture and Markets (NYSDAM) to construct a stormwater management wetland and other
water quality and drainage improvements at Muscoot Farm. The WAC will fund the balance of the project costs. Upon successful completion of the construction, the
SWCD will transfer the full amount of the state grant to the WAC. Term: 07/15/20 - 12/31/21. Positions: included in the Operating Budget.
Prior Year Current Year Federal State Adv. to Grants Other
Meadow Restoration at Croton Park $500,000
Acct T-794 19-794R. Granting Agency: NYSDEC. State Sen. Terrence Murphy helped secure $500,000 in state funding to implement a large meadow restoration project
atop the former Croton Landfill in Croton Point. Park. The state funding will be administered by Planning in cooperation with PRC and DEF. PRC, through the
Westchester Parks Foundation, had hired a firm to conceptually design and develop management specifications for restoration of approximately 100 acres of meadow or
grassland atop the former landfill. These were completed in a report dated 10/19/15. This firm roughly estimates the overall project cost (design and construction) at
$600K to $650K. The state funding, therefore, will provide bulk of the project cost. To help implement the full project as designed, federal funding provided to the SWCD
will be used to fund the balance of the project costs. The SWCD is using $130,000 of these funds to hire a firm to convert the project's conceptual plans and
specifications to construction-level plans and specifications in accordance with County standards and any balance will be used towards the project's construction. Term:
08/01/16 - 10/31/21. Positions: included in the Operating Budget.
Prior Year Current Year Federal State Adv. to Grants Other
EOH Stormwater Retrofit O&M $750,000
Acct T-W18 19W-W018 Granting Agency: NYC Dept. of Environmental Protection. The County has entered into an Agreement with the East of Hudson Watershed
Corporation that will provide up to $750,000 in East of Hudson Water Quality Investment Program funds to fund O&M for Year 1-5 of the EOHWC Retrofit Program.
Term: 01/01/16 - 12/31/22. Positions: included in the Operating Budget.
Prior Year Current Year Federal State Adv. to Grants Other
Bedford Sewer Project $6,500,000
Acct T-W17 19W-W017. Granting Agency: NYC Dept. of Environmental Protection. The County has entered into an Intermunicipal Agreement with the Town of Bedford
that will provide up to $6,500,000 in East of Hudson Water Quality Investment Program funds to fund the construction of the Katonah/Bedford Hills Sewer Project. Term:
04/27/17-04/26/22. Positions: included in the Operating Budget.
Prior Year Current Year Federal State Adv. to Grants Other
Water Quality Study - Bronx River Watershed $245,428
Acct T-1011 T11U. Granting Agency: NYSDEC. Award to study the environmental health of the Bronx River and its watershed. The Soil and Water District will hire a
consultant to study and make recommendations on future projects for the Bronx River. The state funding, therefore, will provide bulk of the project cost. The SWCD will
match the grant with $25,000. Term: 09/01/18 - 08/31/23. Positions: included in the Operating Budget.

Emergency Services (20)
Mission Statement
The mission of the Department of Emergency Services is to protect lives, property, and the environment in Westchester County through the
provision of comprehensive Training, Communication Services, Mitigation, Emergency Preparedness, Response and Management to support
municipalities and emergency service agencies. The Department supports first responders as we work together to build, sustain, and improve
our capability to mitigate, prepare for, protect against, respond to, and recover from all natural and human caused hazards.
The Fire Services Division focuses on fire training, fire inspection, special operations, fire prevention and protection. The division administers,
coordinates, and maintains the fire training program, and manages the County Fire Mutual Aid Plans and Program.
The Emergency Communications Center provides primary dispatch services for 53 fire departments and 31 EMS agencies in Westchester.
The center is staffed 24 hours, seven days a week to dispatch and handle fire and EMS mutual aid requests.
The Emergency Medical Services division collaborates with local, regional, and state agencies to enhance the effectiveness and the county's
Emergency Medical Services System, by providing emergency preparedness training, mutual aid coordination, and incident management
assistance during large-scale events.
The Office of Emergency Management works with local, county , state and federal government agencies before, during, and after natural and
human caused emergencies to mitigate, prepare for, respond to, and recover from emergencies and disasters. During a major emergency ,
OEM may activate the county's Emergency Operations Center, a state-of-the-art facility where the county, the State, municipalities, hospitals,
utilities, and others work together to keep the County safe.

Emergency Services (20)
The Department of Emergency Services is responsible for several programs relating to fire and emergency medical services training and
mutual aid, in addition to disaster preparedness and response. The Department of Emergency Services performs these under the following
state laws and regulations.
Programs and Services State Law or Regulations
Arson Investigation, hazardous materials incident response plan NYS General Municipal Law, Section 204
